name: Enroll member in promotion by member ID and promotion ID
description: Documented success response (Sample JSON Response, lines 22950-22976) for enrolling a member into a promotion. Inner outputParameters.results contains the enrolled MemberId and the newly created LoyaltyProgramMbrPromotionId. Request shape matches the documented member-ID + promotion-ID example (lines 22929-22937).
notes:
AUTHORITATIVE: All fields, paths, method, and version taken verbatim from Loyalty Management Developer Guide v67.0. Resource definition + HTTP methods/Available version at lines 22596-22634; "Enroll for Promotions" section (Example URL, Sample JSON Requests, Sample JSON Response) at lines 22914-22980.

ORG-CONFIGURED: The doc explicitly states (line 22922-22923) that the request and response parameters vary depending on the input/output parameters configured for the EnrollInPromotion process type in a Salesforce org. So the SET of request fields (MemberId/PromotionId vs MembershipNumber/PromotionName, and any other inputs) and the SET of response result fields are implementer-defined. The two documented request variants and the single documented response shape are reproduced exactly; treat the specific field set as a documented EXAMPLE rather than a fixed contract.

REQUEST WRAPPER: Standard processParameters (typed "Loyalty Process Parameter Map" in the property table, lines 22626-22629) — an array of objects. Required.

RESPONSE SCHEMA: Named "Loyalty Process Output" (line 22633). The guide does not provide a separate Response Bodies property table for it; the shape is documented only via the Sample JSON Response (message:null, outputParameters.outputParameters.results[], simulationDetails:{}, status:true). The doubly-nested outputParameters.outputParameters is exactly as printed in the doc, not a transcription artifact.

VERSION: "Available version" is 56.0 (line 22599); the example URL uses /services/data/v67.0/ (the guide's current version). available is set to the documented "Available version" 56.0; sfPath uses v67.0 as shown in the Example URL.

IDS: Used the exact documented Salesforce IDs from the sample JSON (MemberId 0lMRM0000004GBn2AM, LoyaltyProgramMbrPromotionId 1lpRM0000004CEmYAM; request placeholder IDs 0lMxx00000000BJEAY / 0c8xx00000000BJEAY as shown).

NO ERROR FIXTURE: The doc shows no error/failure JSON example for this process, so only success fixtures were created. Second fixture reuses the single documented success response under the alternative documented request form.

OPEN QUESTION: A real org may return additional/different result fields or a status:false / message-populated error envelope; those shapes are not documented for this specific process and were therefore not fabricated.
